data.title: Salesforce (NYSE: CRM) declares $0.416 quarterly cash dividend per share, payable on Jan 8, 2026 to shareholders of record on Dec 18, 2025.
data.description: In San Francisco, Salesforce (NYSE: CRM) revealed the declaration of a quarterly cash dividend of $0.416 per share by its board of directors. Shareholders of record on December 18, 2025, will receive the dividend on January 8, 2026. Salesforce is known as the world's top AI CRM, assisting organizations in transforming into agentic enterprises by unifying humans, agents, apps, and data on a trusted platform for exceptional growth and innovation. Learn more at www.salesforce.com.

data.title: SOL price held at $140 amid competition from new altcoin ETFs, affecting demand with declining activity and leverage.
data.description: Solana (SOL) is facing difficulties in maintaining its recent gains as a result of decreased activity, reduced demand for leverage, and the emergence of competing spot altcoin ETFs. These factors have hindered Solana's price rebound, posing challenges for the cryptocurrency.

data.title: Bitcoin, Ethereum, XRP, Dogecoin prices drop. Bitcoin at $92,460.85, Ethereum $3,156.33, XRP $2.10, Dogecoin $0.1480. Trading volume falls 19%. Liquidations hit $266 million.
data.description: Major cryptocurrencies experienced a decline amidst expectations of interest rate cuts. At 8:25 p.m. ET, Bitcoin fell by 1.04% to $92,460.85, Ethereum dropped by 1.77% to $3,156.33, XRP decreased by 4.30% to $2.10, Solana fell by 4.40% to $139.58, and Dogecoin decreased by 2.75% to $0.1480. Bitcoin consolidated in the $91,000-$92,000 range with a 19% decrease in trading volume. Ethereum faced strong rejection near $3,220 and dropped to $3,070 before rebounding. Cryptocurrency-linked stocks like Strategy Inc. and Coinbase Global Inc. closed down by 1.26% and 1.04% during regular trading. Cryptocurrency liquidations amounted to $266 million in the last 24 hours, with long liquidations nearing $80 million. Over $440 million in Bitcoin short positions faced the risk of liquidation if Bitcoin surpassed $95,000.
Hyundai Motor’s record-setting rally on U.S. tariff relief lifts South Korean equities.

data.title: China's 15th Five-Year Plan to offer global growth opportunities. Plan for 2026-30 aims to boost China's development.
data.description: The recent unveiling of China's 15th Five-Year Plan, scheduled for 2026-2030, is expected to inject new energy into global growth. The plan, announced in Beijing, aims to propel the country's development while creating substantial opportunities for advancement. With a target timeframe spanning five years, from 2026 to 2030, the plan signifies China's strategic vision for the future and holds promise for innovative initiatives that could impact various sectors on a global scale.

data.title: Leon Stok from IBM elected Chair of Si2 Board for 2025–2026 term. Has been on Si2 Board since 2007. Drives design and verification at IBM.
data.description: In Austin, Texas, the Silicon Integration Initiative (Si2) declared that Dr. Leon Stok, VP of Electronic Design Automation at IBM, has been selected as Chair of the Si2 Board of Directors for the remainder of the 2025–2026 term. Dr. Stok, a Si2 Board Member since 2007 and former Board Chair, supervises a team at IBM that provides top-notch design and verification flows and tools for the creation of semiconductor products.

data.title: Meta's stocks rise as reports suggest a potential 30% cut in metaverse budget, particularly in Reality Labs. Shareholders back the move.
data.description: Reportedly, Meta is losing interest in the metaverse and is set to reduce its virtual reality research department, Reality Labs, by 30%. Shareholders appear to be in favor of this decision.
